calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-06-2024 Off Calendar

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-29-2024 Continued To May-07-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion For Payment Of Attorneys Fees And Reimbursement Of Costs And Expenses
Off Calendar Mar-15-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. INGA MIDDLETON, Esq of Hawkins Parnell & Young, LLP (tel: 213-486-8000) for Defendant.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF FRANCISCO LOPEZ, OMAR AGUILAR's Motion For Payment Of Attorneys Fees And Reimbursement Of Costs And Expenses.

Matter not argued/contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Francisco Bravo Lopez's motion for payment of attorney's fees and reimbursement of costs and expenses is granted in the amount of $37,000 in attorney's fees and $2,496.36 in costs and expenses (Total = $39,496.36).

The court employs the lodestar method to determine plaintiff's reasonable fees. "Under the lodestar adjustment methodology, the trial court must initially determine the actual time expended and then 'ascertain whether under all the circumstances of the case the amount of actual time expended and the monetary charge being made for the time expended are reasonable.'" (Mikhaeilpoor v. BMW of North America, LLC (2020) 48 Cal.App.5th 240, 247.) The court concludes that most of the claimed fees are reasonable. "Where a party continues to litigate after receiving a settlement offer, absent a finding that failure to resolve the case through negotiation was unreasonable or solely attributable to counsel's desire to generate more fees, additional fees incurred to establish liability or damages, including evidence of willfulness necessary to recover civil penalties, are properly included in an award of fees under Civil Code section 1794, subdivision (d)." (Hanna v. Mercedes-Benz USA, LLC (2019) 36 Cal.App.5th 493, 508.)

Plaintiff presents evidence that its billing rates for this contingency case in San Francisco are reasonable. (Myers Decl., pars. 65, 66, 68, 69.) The court, however, concludes that this relatively routine case was overstaffed. (Morris v. Hyundai Motor America (2019) 41 Cal.App.5th 24, 39 ["it is appropriate for a trial court to reduce a fee award based on its reasonable determination that a routine, noncomplex case was overstaffed to a degree that significant inefficiencies and inflated fees resulted."].) Plaintiff had five attorneys working on this case which led to duplication of effort and other inefficiencies. For example, attorney "JM" performed the client intake and attorney "SP" drafted the complaint. (Myers Decl., Ex. A [7/19/22 time entry].) Attorneys "JY," "JS," and "BB" performed repetitive work regarding defendant's fee offer. [Id. [6/5/23 - 6/19/23 time entries].) The claimed fees are therefore reduced.

The court declines to award a positive multiplier.

"The Supreme Court has set forth a number of factors the trial court may consider in adjusting the lodestar figure. These include: (1) the novelty and difficulty of the questions involved, and the skill displayed in presenting them; (2) the extent to which the nature of the litigation precluded other employment by the attorneys; [and] (3) the contingent nature of the fee award, both from the point of view of eventual victory on the merits and the point of view of establishing eligibility for an award." (Mikhaeilpoor, 48 Cal.App.5th at 248 (internal quotations omitted).)

While plaintiff took this case on a contingency, it was not complex or novel. (See Hall v. FCA US LLC (E.D. Cal. May 21, 2018) 2018 WL 2298431 at *8 [declining to award multiplier in Song-Beverly Act case where, among other things, case did not present novel or difficult questions of law or fact, there was no evidence that the nature of the litigation precluded other employment by the attorneys, and the contingent nature of the fee award was outweighed by the other factors].)

Order signed.

Judge: Rochelle C. East; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RCE)
